candidates.why: Direct top-line revenue line at top of income statement; identical to Total Revenue in preview, so use as preferred label.
Explicit total revenue label; same values as Sales and useful as fallback across years.

variables.notes: Use Sales first, with Total Revenue as fallback because both appear to represent the same top-line item across years.

candidates.why: Best match for direct production/service costs; sits in cost section under revenue.
Could be a component of cost of sales, but preview is empty and appears subordinate to Materials and services.
Balancing row, not appropriate when a better direct cost line exists.

variables.notes: Selected the broad direct-cost line rather than subordinate or balancing rows.

candidates.why: Clear operating overhead bucket excluding depreciation and finance items.
Could be overhead in some firms, but here likely part of Materials and services/COGS structure, so excluded.
Dedicated R&D row exists but only appears in supplemental section below operating profit, so do not include in XSGA per rule.

variables.notes: No explicit SG&A total row exists. Chose the best overhead components and excluded depreciation, COGS-related rows, and the below-EBIT supplemental R&D row.

candidates.why: Dedicated R&D label exists. It appears in the supplemental section below operating profit, so it can still map to XRD but should not be included in XSGA.

variables.notes: Mapped the dedicated R&D line to XRD only. Not added to XSGA because it appears below operating profit in the statement order.

candidates.why: Most semantically precise recurring financing-cost label.
Broader finance-cost line, but using it alongside Interest Expense risks double counting.

variables.notes: Preferred the specific interest label over broader Financial Expense to avoid potential double counting. The row is present in the sheet, so it should be considered valid even if preview cells are empty.

candidates.why: Best match for book equity and appears to exclude non-controlling interests based on the component rows shown.
Includes minority interest, so not preferred when MIB is mapped separately.

variables.notes: Selected Total Equity because it best represents parent/common equity and avoids double counting with separate MIB.

candidates.why: Direct balance-sheet line for minority interest; preferred wording.
Alternative label for the same concept in other years.
Income statement allocation line, not the balance-sheet stock measure preferred for MIB.

variables.notes: Used balance-sheet NCI labels as a priority list for minority interest across years.
